modules.module_title: NATP Module 1: Introduction to Nurse Assistant
modules.source_status_flag: Generated from approved Module 1 PDF source map.
learning_objectives: Discuss the legal implications of California Code of Regulations, Title 22, Division 5, and OBRA
Identify Nurse Assistant requirements as set forth in Title 22, and OBRA
Describe the qualities of a successful Nurse Assistant
Describe the role and responsibilities of the Nurse Assistant
Describe professionalism for the Nurse Assistant.
Explain the ethical behavior expected of the Nurse Assistant

media_prompt_placeholder.alt_text: Training visual: Objective 1 Overview. De-identified illustration; no PHI.

Review the approved source outline, use de-identified examples only, and apply report/observe/defer boundaries. Source excerpt support: Identify Nurse Assistant requirements as set forth in Title 22 and OBRA A. Complete Nurse Assistant training course approved by the California Department of Public Health (CDPH) 1. Minimum of 60 hours of mandated theory, including 10 required hours for Alzheimer's Disease and Elder Abuse 2. Minimum of 100 hours of clinical training in a skilled nursing facility 3. Course content must comply with Title 22 and OBRA 4. Students who are employees of a skilled nursing facility must be paid an hourly

Review the approved source outline, use de-identified examples only, and apply report/observe/defer boundaries. Source excerpt support: A. Definition of professionalism. Carrying out patient/resident care in a manner that is: 1. Ethical 2. Competent 3. Knowledgeable 4. Caring 5. Committed 6. Representative of high standards B. Professional behaviors or attitudes 1. Being skilled and caring while performing care 2. Being responsible, trustworthy, truthful (e.g., reporting change of address to CDPH, keeping a record of in-service education/CEU's) 3. Attending in-service/educational activities to improve skills and knowledge 4. Sho

cards.transcript_text: Objective 8: Describe behaviors that maintain confidentiality. This dry-run lesson is mapped to Module 1: Introduction to Nurse Assistant and remains within CNA theory/support boundaries.

cards.learning_goal: Describe behaviors that maintain confidentiality
cards.learner_facing_content: Source-traced focus: Describe behaviors that maintain confidentiality. Review the approved source outline, use de-identified examples only, and apply report/observe/defer boundaries. Source excerpt support: Describe behaviors that maintain confidentiality. A. Confidentiality can be maintained by discussing personal information only with appropriate health team members at appropriate times and appropriate places Model Curriculum for Nurse Assistants - Revised December 2018 B. Examples of maintaining confidentiality Module 1: Introduction to Nurse Assistant 1. Adhering to HIPAA guidelines 2. Discussing observations with nurse or patient's/resident's online. physician https://www.hhs.gov/hip 3. Discus
